tldr: About a dozen of your live ads are running the same wording word-for-word, and most of them have been up for 70+ days. Meta sees these near-copies as basically one ad, so it quietly picks one and stops showing the rest.

benchmark.context: The typical brand your size swaps in fresh ads every 3 weeks. The best brands do it every 2 weeks. You have ads still running at 76, 86, and 89 days old with the same words, which is well past where they stop pulling their weight.

ads.headline: No Fake Sugars. Just Real Hydration
ads.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 0 calories, and real ingredients. Try all fan-favorite flavors in the Variety Pack - your new go-to refreshment.
drivers: This exact headline and body appear word-for-word across at least six of your live ads.
It has been running 89 days, more than four times your practical refresh point.
Meta treats these near-identical ads as one, so you paid to make ads almost no one is seeing.

ads.headline: Zero Sugar. Full Flavor.
ads.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 0 calories, and real ingredients. Try all fan-favorite flavors in the Variety Pack - your new go-to refreshment.
drivers: The body copy is a straight copy of your 'No Fake Sugars' ad with only the headline swapped.
89 days old, so anyone likely to click this has already scrolled past it dozens of times.
A different headline on the same body doesn't count as a new ad to Meta, so it gets shown to fewer people.

ads.headline: No Fake Sugars. Just Real Hydration
ads.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 0 calories, and real ingredients. Try all fan-favorite flavors in the Variety Pack - your new go-to refreshment.
drivers: Same headline and body as several other live ads, only the page it sends people to is different.
89 days running with no changes to the words or the look.
Splitting spend across copies of the same ad means none of them build up momentum.

ads.headline: 20% Off First Order💸🍯
ads.body: Swap the syrupy stuff for flavor that hits different. Strive Hydration Soda. Order today.🥤
drivers: The body is so short it doesn't say what the drink actually is or why anyone should care.
89 days old, and a duplicate of this same ad is also running.
The '20% off' offer here clashes with your other live ads promising '15% off' and 'code HONEY20', so buyers see mixed prices.

ads.headline: WHY EVERYONE IS REACHING FOR STRIVE RIGHT NOW
ads.body: Some drinks feel refreshing at first… then leave you feeling heavy. Strive was made to feel different. Sweetened with honey and infused with electrolytes for hydration, it delivers crisp carbonation and balanced flavor in a lighter soda you can actually enjoy. Refreshing, easy to drink, and designed for active days. That's why more people are reaching for Strive.
drivers: You have three copies of this same ad running with the exact same words at the same time.
The all-caps headline reads like a magazine cover, not a real reason to buy.
76 days in, and Meta is picking one copy to serve and hiding the other two.

ads_compact.headline: Get 15% OFF now
ads_compact.body: Win $100+ Worth of Strive Soda + Get 15% OFF Today. Here's how it works: enter your email, get 15% off instantly, then place your first order to automatically enter the quarterly drawing.

ads_compact.headline: No Fake Sugars. Just Real Hydration
ads_compact.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 45 calories, and real ingredients.

ads_compact.headline: Real Flavor, Real Hydration
ads_compact.body: You shouldn't have to choose between amazing taste and better ingredients. Strive is the hydration soda with crave-worthy flavor, 100% organic wildflower honey, and electrolytes for everyday movement.

hero_concept.hook: The soda you can drink after a run.
hero_concept.headline: Peach, honey, electrolytes.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Most sodas leave you heavy. Ours doesn't.

Strive Peach is sweetened only with organic wildflower honey and carries 180mg of electrolytes in every can. 45 calories. No sucralose, no aspartame, no stevia, no high-fructose corn syrup.

One sip and you'll get why over 100,000 people made the swap. Try the Peach 12-pack today.
hero_concept.visual_direction: A single chilled Peach can on a sunlit kitchen counter beside a small honey dipper and fresh peach slices, condensation beading on the can, bright and clean.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Almost every ad you're running right now leans on the same generic 'zero sugar' line and pushes the variety pack. Nothing zooms in on a single flavor with a real, specific reason to try it, which is why the whole set is starting to blur together.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a sunlit kitchen counter scene with a small wooden honey dipper drizzling honey onto a white ceramic dish and two fresh ripe peach slices arranged beside the can, with soft natural morning window light casting gentle shadows and highlighting the condensation beading on the aluminum surface. Clean, bright, wholesome aesthetic with warm peach and honey tones balanced by a crisp white counter surface. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact peach-and-cream colorway of the Strive Soda Peach can with its exact printed branding, exact 'Strive' wordmark, exact 'Peach' flavor label, exact honey/peach graphic elements, and exact nutrition/ingredient callouts as shown on the reference can - strictly NO color shift to orange, red, or yellow, NO altering the wordmark, NO changing the flavor name, NO removing or modifying any printed text or graphics on the can. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction: a single standard 12oz slim aluminum soda can, one can only, upright orientation, standard pull-tab top, full-wrap printed label with all branding intact including the Strive logo, the 'Peach' flavor callout, and any electrolyte/honey/calorie callouts printed on the can. Do NOT add or remove cans, do NOT change can size or shape, do NOT alter the pull-tab, do NOT remove or replace any printed text or graphic on the label, do NOT introduce a bottle or glass container in place of the can. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: The dozen near-identical variety-pack ads are already burning out and will fully stall within the next 5-11 days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Strive Soda refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Christopher, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads, the pictures, the words, and how long each one has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
